title: haloragis acutangula
intro: Haloragis acutangula, also known as the water milfoil or water ragwort, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating skin irritations. The herbal preparations of Haloragis acutangula include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
